metrics.narrative: Buyer quantified payment operations pain: currently executing 30-40 manual payment runs per week across 140 bank accounts, a time-consuming and error-prone process they want to eliminate through automation (source: 3 June call per prior record). This is the #1 operational pain and the primary driver for evaluating a payment-integrated P2P solution. No ROI or cost-savings figure articulated, but the manual workload is severe enough that the buyer explicitly stated payment automation is the must-have capability ('I thought you guys could do payments and I think that was a real downer' when learning Zycus lacks native payment module, per 3 June call). Additional pain: consolidating purchasing, company cards, reimbursable expenses, and payments into 'one solution for all purchasing' to replace fragmented D365 + xFlow + SK Global stack (27 May call per prior record). Quantifying the hard ROI (FTE hours saved, error-rate reduction, cost per payment run) should be a priority move to strengthen the business case and justify budget allocation.

competition.narrative: Four named competitors, two procurement-focused (Zip, Zycus), two payment-focused (Renpayments, Payhawk/Airwallix). Primary competitive threat: Renpayments (Ren), currently running CFO-sponsored 2-week POC as of mid-June (per Next Step ~15 June 'The CFO is looking into Renpayments - currently running a 2 week POC'), outcome unknown, concludes imminently (late June/early July). If Ren POC succeeds, CFO may favor Ren regardless of David/Robert's Zycus preference. Ren positioning unknown (payment-only platform or end-to-end P2P + payments?). Secondary threat: Zip, procurement-focused platform that 'does look good and similar but slightly different' (per 24 June per prior record), directly competing on Zycus's differentiated strength (purchasing UX, AI). If Zip's AI capability and roadmap are comparable to Zycus and price is lower, Zip may win the procurement-finalist slot. Tertiary threats: Payhawk and Airwallix, payment-focused platforms (per prior record), likely competing on payment automation strength where Zycus is exposed (no native payment module). Also mentioned: Hughes, Yooz, Quadian (per prior record, status unknown, likely eliminated or lower priority). Incumbent: D365 + xFlow + SK Global (displacement, not renewal). Competitive wedge: Zycus's strength is end-to-end P2P + AI (Merlin Intake guided purchasing, ANA approval intelligence), but payment gap is fatal unless partner integration (Transfermate or other) is priced competitively and delivered reliably. How we win: (1) de-position Zip on AI depth (Merlin negotiation agent, I2O roadmap) and offer to build missing features free pre-contract; (2) price Zycus + payment partner integration below £250k threshold and below Ren all-in cost; (3) position Ren as payment-only (if true) vs Zycus end-to-end P2P + payments; (4) secure CFO overlay (Anthony Gray) to counter Ren POC momentum before decision early July. Competitive review not conducted in 30+ days despite Ren POC active and Zip advancement.

decision_criteria.narrative: Buyer's evaluation criteria emerged across discovery and demo calls. Primary must-have: native or integrated payment automation to eliminate 30-40 manual weekly payment runs and route payments across 140 bank accounts (confirmed 3 June call per prior record). Zycus lacks native payment module, so partner integration cost and feasibility is a decisive criterion. Secondary criteria: end-to-end P2P automation including guided intake, multi-level PO approvals, 3-way invoice matching with exception routing (perfect match, mismatch over/under PO, no PO, multi-invoice to one PO), budget commitment impact visibility, and D365 ERP integration (confirmed per Next Step demo scope 'guided intake, PO approval, invoice automation, approval context, exception routing, and D365 integration'). Buyer also wants expenses management consolidated into one platform ('one solution for all purchasing… PO supplies company cards reimbursable expenses payments' per 27 May call), but stated 'we can still implement another solution to do expenses… the main focus is purchasing' (3 June call), so expenses is a nice-to-have, not a blocker. AI capability is implied in product scope (Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA in Products field) but no explicit AI criteria articulated in available evidence. RFP process: CFO reviewing 2-3 demos, budget ~£250k, timeline decision early July (per Next Step). Vendor selection will likely hinge on payment solution viability and total cost (Zycus + partner integration) vs all-in pricing from payment-native competitors (Renpayments, Payhawk, Airwallix).

Offer to build any Zip feature Yondr values, free, pre-contract (Nishita commitment 24 June). Provide a side-by-side AI capability matrix (autonomous negotiation, touchless invoicing, conversational interface) with customer proof points. Timing: this must land before shortlist decision (week of 30 June) to ensure Zycus is the procurement finalist, not Zip.

Deliver the commercial pack by Friday 27 June at the lowest defensible price to secure first-mover advantage and finalist slot: Buyer stated 'our budget is zero… get it as cheap as you can' and 'we haven't actually received any prices from any of the potential solutions yet' (24 June). Price is explicitly a decision factor in a zero-budget environment post-ERP. Zycus has opportunity to anchor the buyer's price expectation by being first to submit a compelling commercial (software license for P2P suite ideally under the £250k RFP threshold noted in Next_Step__c, plus transparent implementation cost). Action: Claire to finalize and send commercial pack including payment partner integration options and total cost of ownership by end of Friday 27 June as committed on 24 June call. Emphasize value (AI, feature build, master-data integration, references) to justify price and offer flexible payment terms if needed to fit budget constraints.

Secure Anthony Gray (the deal owner's manager, Zycus VP Europe) for CFO (Sandy) demo if Zycus is shortlisted to final two, to build exec-level relationship and de-risk commercial negotiation: The CFO is the Economic Buyer and final decision-maker (stated 3 June and 24 June). David and Robert are the evaluators and will recommend two vendors, but CFO approval is the gate. Anthony Gray was on the discovery call 27 May and holds the regional exec relationship. Buyer has stated 'our CFO… we will decide who we put in front of him' (24 June). Action: if Zycus is shortlisted (known early July), offer an exec briefing or demo with Anthony Gray + Claire + Nishita for the CFO, positioned as strategic partnership discussion (scale, roadmap, commitment to build features, long-term P2P vision). This de-risks price negotiation (exec can offer flexibility), builds C-level confidence (peer-to-peer), and differentiates Zycus from vendor-only demos. Timing: offer this in the commercial pack or immediately upon shortlist confirmation.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is competing in a four-finalist rapid evaluation (Zycus, Zip, Renpayments, Payhawk/Airwallix) for a P2P + payment automation deal where payment capability is the buyer's #1 pain and Zycus lacks a native payment module. Primary threat is Renpayments (CFO-sponsored POC concluding imminently, outcome unknown). Secondary threat is Zip (procurement-focused, similar to Zycus, price-competitive). Zycus strengths: end-to-end P2P, AI (Merlin/ANA), operational advocate (David + team favor Zycus per Next Step 'Rest of the team really like Zycus'). Zycus vulnerabilities: no native payment module (buyer stated disappointment 3 June), payment platform in development with no launch date or reference customers, mitigation is partner integration (adds cost, may push total above £250k budget threshold), and no CFO engagement despite CFO running Ren POC. Competitive wedge: position Zycus as end-to-end P2P + AI + payment partner integration vs payment-only platforms (Ren, Payhawk, Airwallix) and vs Zip on AI depth. How we win: price competitively (Zycus + partner integration under £250k and under Ren all-in cost), de-position Zip on AI roadmap and offer to build missing features free, position Ren as payment-only (if true) vs Zycus breadth, and secure CFO overlay (Anthony Gray) to counter Ren POC momentum before decision early July. No competitive review conducted in 30+ days despite active POC and finalist advancement.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI scope is in product mix (Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA per Products field), suggesting Zycus positioned AI-driven intake and approval intelligence, but no explicit buyer articulation of AI needs, appetite, or evaluation criteria in available evidence. No AIS score/status/why fields populated (all null per ground truth), so AI excitement is unknown and cannot be assessed. Buyer pain is operational (manual payment runs, fragmented systems), not explicitly AI-driven, though AI intake/approval could address workflow inefficiency. Competitor Zip is also AI-capable ('does look good and similar' per 24 June), so AI is not a unique differentiator unless Zycus demonstrates superior AI depth (Merlin negotiation agent, I2O agentic purchasing, ANA approval intelligence). No evidence of Zycus articulating AI roadmap, Merlin capabilities, or I2O vision in demos or calls. AI positioning appears present but under-leveraged: product scope includes AI but buyer has not been sold on AI as a decisive criterion. Recommended action: position Merlin/I2O narrative aggressively in workshop and commercial discussions to differentiate from Zip and payment-focused competitors, and tie AI capability to buyer's operational pain (e.g. AI-driven payment routing across 140 bank accounts, AI approval intelligence to reduce exception handling).

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Buyer expectations are highly aligned on P2P scope (guided intake, PO approval, invoice automation, exception routing, D365 integration) where Zycus is strong, but critically misaligned on payment automation (buyer's #1 pain) where Zycus is exposed. Buyer wants native or seamlessly integrated payment capability to eliminate 30-40 manual weekly payment runs across 140 bank accounts. Zycus lacks native payment module; payment platform is in development with no launch date or reference customers (per Nishita 3 June per prior record). Mitigation offered is partner integration (Transfermate or other), but this adds cost and complexity and buyer has zero budget ('get it as cheap as you can' per 24 June). Buyer also wants expenses management consolidated but stated it is not a blocker ('we can still implement another solution to do expenses… the main focus is purchasing' per 3 June). AI positioning (Merlin/ANA) is in scope (Products field) but buyer has not articulated explicit AI needs or appetite in available evidence, so AI fit is assumed aligned but unconfirmed. Overall fit: strong on P2P breadth and operational workflow, exposed on payment capability (the decisive criterion), and competitive on price only if partner integration cost is absorbed or minimized.

hard.ais_why: Yondr has a strong appetite for AI-led P2P transformation, especially around guided intake, invoice automation, approval visibility, and reducing AP manual effort. The VP Finance specifically wants to see a solution users can “talk to,” while the team is looking for AI-focused automation to simplify PO creation, improve compliance, and scale without adding AP headcount.

Zycus has no native expenses management module. Buyer wants 'one solution for all purchasing… PO supplies company cards reimbursable expenses payments' (27 May). Expenses currently in D365; buyer would prefer to consolidate but stated 'we can still implement another solution to do expenses… the main focus is purchasing' (3 June), so this is acceptable gap, lower risk than payments gap.

De-position Zip on AI maturity and feature roadmap before CFO demos (early July): Zip is the only other procurement-platform finalist and the direct peer threat on Zycus's differentiated strength (purchasing UX, AI). Buyer stated Zip 'does look good and similar but slightly different' (24 June). Action: in the commercial pack (due 27 June) or a follow-up call, contrast Zycus 714 agentic agents (live, customer-proven per Coca-Cola Porsche Cargoitalia references from discovery deck) against Zip's intake-orchestration positioning (per Claire's note Zip is 'bolt-on' not full platform). Offer to build any Zip feature Yondr values, free, pre-contract (Nishita commitment 24 June). Provide a side-by-side AI capability matrix (autonomous negotiation, touchless invoicing, conversational interface) with customer proof points. Timing: this must land before shortlist decision (week of 30 June) to ensure Zycus is the procurement finalist, not Zip.

Deliver the commercial pack by Friday 27 June at the lowest defensible price to secure first-mover advantage and finalist slot: Buyer stated 'our budget is zero… get it as cheap as you can' and 'we haven't actually received any prices from any of the potential solutions yet' (24 June). Price is explicitly a decision factor in a zero-budget environment post-ERP. Zycus has opportunity to anchor the buyer's price expectation by being first to submit a compelling commercial (software license for P2P suite ideally under the £250k RFP threshold noted in Next_Step__c, plus transparent implementation cost). Action: Claire to finalize and send commercial pack including payment partner integration options and total cost of ownership by end of Friday 27 June as committed on 24 June call. Emphasize value (AI, feature build, master-data integration, references) to justify price and offer flexible payment terms if needed to fit budget constraints.

Secure Anthony Gray (the deal owner's manager, Zycus VP Europe) for CFO (Sandy) demo if Zycus is shortlisted to final two, to build exec-level relationship and de-risk commercial negotiation: The CFO is the Economic Buyer and final decision-maker (stated 3 June and 24 June). David and Robert are the evaluators and will recommend two vendors, but CFO approval is the gate. Anthony Gray was on the discovery call 27 May and holds the regional exec relationship. Buyer has stated 'our CFO… we will decide who we put in front of him' (24 June). Action: if Zycus is shortlisted (known early July), offer an exec briefing or demo with Anthony Gray + Claire + Nishita for the CFO, positioned as strategic partnership discussion (scale, roadmap, commitment to build features, long-term P2P vision). This de-risks price negotiation (exec can offer flexibility), builds C-level confidence (peer-to-peer), and differentiates Zycus from vendor-only demos. Timing: offer this in the commercial pack or immediately upon shortlist confirmation.
